Recommended_Architecture: Flat structure with main.go and handlers/
Error_Strategy: Sentinel errors with fmt.Errorf wrapping
Concurrency_Strategy: Single goroutine per request; use context for cancellation
Testing_Strategy: Table-driven tests with httptest.NewServer
Anti_Patterns: God handler functions, no input validation, ignoring context cancellation

Recommended_Architecture: Clean Architecture: cmd/ internal/handler/ internal/service/ internal/repository/ pkg/
Package_Priority: chi or gin, sqlx or pgx, log/slog, otel
Error_Strategy: Custom error types implementing error interface with error codes
Concurrency_Strategy: Worker pool pattern for background tasks; errgroup for parallel requests
Testing_Strategy: Integration tests with testcontainers; unit tests with mocks via interfaces
Anti_Patterns: Circular dependencies between packages, database logic in handlers, global state

Recommended_Architecture: Layered: cmd/ internal/handler/ internal/service/ internal/repository/ migrations/
Package_Priority: pgx/v5, golang-migrate, squirrel or raw SQL, log/slog
Error_Strategy: Wrap pgx errors into domain errors; detect constraint violations via error codes
Concurrency_Strategy: Connection pool via pgxpool; use context deadlines on all queries
Testing_Strategy: Test with testcontainers-go/postgres; use txdb for transaction-based test isolation
Anti_Patterns: N+1 queries, missing indexes, not closing rows, raw string concatenation in SQL

Error_Strategy: Wrap mongo errors; handle mongo.ErrNoDocuments explicitly
Concurrency_Strategy: Use connection pooling via mongo.Client; avoid opening multiple clients
Testing_Strategy: Test with testcontainers-go/mongodb; seed test data in setup functions
Anti_Patterns: Storing highly relational data, unbounded array growth in documents, missing indexes on query fields

Error_Strategy: Return GraphQL-specific errors with extensions; map domain errors to GQL error codes
Concurrency_Strategy: Use dataloaders to batch and cache N+1 queries; errgroup for parallel field resolution
Testing_Strategy: Test resolvers as unit tests with mock services; integration tests against schema
Anti_Patterns: Exposing database schema directly as GraphQL schema, unbounded query depth, missing query complexity limits

Recommended_Architecture: Single main.go with cobra or flag package
Error_Strategy: Print errors to stderr with os.Exit(1); use fmt.Errorf for wrapping
Concurrency_Strategy: Not needed; sequential execution
Testing_Strategy: Test with exec.Command or by extracting logic into testable functions
Anti_Patterns: Printing errors to stdout, using log.Fatal in library code, not setting exit codes

Recommended_Architecture: cmd/ internal/ pkg/ with cobra subcommands
Package_Priority: cobra, viper, bubbletea or lipgloss, log/slog
Error_Strategy: Structured error types per subcommand; aggregate errors for batch operations
Concurrency_Strategy: Use errgroup for parallel file operations; worker pools for batch processing
Testing_Strategy: Test each subcommand independently; test config loading; use golden files for output
Anti_Patterns: Deeply nested cobra commands, mixing UI and business logic, not handling SIGINT gracefully

Recommended_Architecture: cmd/ internal/server/ internal/service/ internal/repository/ proto/ api/
Error_Strategy: Return gRPC status codes; map domain errors to codes.Code; use status.Errorf
Concurrency_Strategy: Per-RPC goroutine managed by gRPC server; use streaming for large payloads
Testing_Strategy: Test with bufconn for in-memory gRPC; integration tests with real server
Anti_Patterns: Huge protobuf messages, not using interceptors for cross-cutting concerns, blocking in stream handlers

Package_Priority: sarama or confluent-kafka-go, watermill, protobuf, log/slog
Error_Strategy: Dead letter queues for failed messages; structured error logging with correlation IDs
Concurrency_Strategy: Consumer groups with partition-level concurrency; bounded worker pools per topic
Testing_Strategy: Test with in-memory pubsub (watermill GoChannel); integration tests with testcontainers kafka
Anti_Patterns: Processing messages without idempotency, unbounded goroutines per message, ignoring consumer lag

Recommended_Architecture: Root package with subpackages; examples/ directory
Package_Priority: No external dependencies preferred; use stdlib only
Error_Strategy: Return errors not log them; use sentinel errors and custom error types; never panic
Concurrency_Strategy: Document goroutine safety; use sync primitives; make concurrency opt-in
Testing_Strategy: 100% test coverage on exported API; example tests for documentation; fuzz tests
Anti_Patterns: Logging to stdout, panicking on errors, exposing internal types, unstable API before v1

Package_Priority: Minimal deps; share types via shared/ package
Error_Strategy: Return wrapped errors with context; avoid logging inside library code
Concurrency_Strategy: Safe for concurrent use or clearly documented as not-safe
Testing_Strategy: Unit tests with mocks; benchmark critical paths
Anti_Patterns: Importing from cmd/ packages, tight coupling to specific infrastructure, missing godoc

Error_Strategy: Collect errors per record; continue processing; report summary at end
Concurrency_Strategy: Fan-out/fan-in with bounded worker pools; use semaphore pattern for resource limits
Testing_Strategy: Test each stage independently; use golden files for transform output
Anti_Patterns: Loading entire files into memory, unbounded parallelism, no progress reporting, silent data loss on errors

Error_Strategy: Circuit breakers on sink failures; backpressure via buffered channels; DLQ for poison pills
Concurrency_Strategy: Pipeline of channels with bounded buffers; graceful drain on shutdown
Testing_Strategy: Test with in-memory channels; load test with synthetic data generators
Anti_Patterns: Unbounded channels causing OOM, dropping messages silently, not handling backpressure, no graceful shutdown

Package_Priority: asynq or machinery, redis, log/slog, otel
Error_Strategy: Retry with exponential backoff; dead letter after max retries; structured error logging
Concurrency_Strategy: Worker pool with configurable concurrency; graceful shutdown with drain
Testing_Strategy: Test handlers as pure functions; integration test with real Redis via testcontainers
Anti_Patterns: Unbounded retries, no idempotency on handlers, losing tasks on crash, no dead letter queue

Error_Strategy: Log errors with job context; alert on consecutive failures; track last success time
Concurrency_Strategy: Mutex to prevent overlapping runs; use context for cancellation on shutdown
Testing_Strategy: Test job functions independently; test schedule parsing; integration test timing
Anti_Patterns: Overlapping job runs, no distributed locking in multi-instance deployments, panicking inside cron handlers

Error_Strategy: Return API Gateway-compatible error responses; log errors with request ID
Concurrency_Strategy: Lambda handles concurrency; minimize goroutines; reuse clients across invocations
Testing_Strategy: Test handler functions locally with events/fixtures; use localstack for integration
Anti_Patterns: Importing heavy SDKs unnecessarily, not reusing clients in init(), large deployment packages, ignoring cold starts

Error_Strategy: Return HTTP status codes with structured JSON error bodies; log to Cloud Logging format
Concurrency_Strategy: Cloud Functions manages concurrency; reuse clients in module-level init
Testing_Strategy: Test with httptest; use emulators for GCP services
Anti_Patterns: Creating clients per request, large dependency trees, not setting memory limits, ignoring function timeout

Error_Strategy: Return ctrl.Result with RequeueAfter on transient errors; set status conditions on CR
Concurrency_Strategy: Controller-runtime manages concurrency; use work queues; limit concurrent reconciles
Testing_Strategy: envtest for integration; use fake client for unit tests; e2e with kind cluster
Anti_Patterns: Reconciling without idempotency, not handling finalizers, missing RBAC permissions, not setting owner references

Error_Strategy: Requeue on transient errors with rate limiting; log permanent failures and skip
Concurrency_Strategy: SharedInformers with work queue pattern; limit concurrent workers
Testing_Strategy: Test with fake clientset; integration with envtest; e2e with kind
Anti_Patterns: Polling API server instead of using informers, not using work queues, missing leader election in multi-replica

Error_Strategy: Flash messages for user-facing errors; log internal errors; custom error pages
Concurrency_Strategy: Standard request-per-goroutine; use context for request-scoped values
Testing_Strategy: Test handlers with httptest; test templates render without errors; browser tests with chromedp
Anti_Patterns: Template injection via unescaped input, not using embed for templates, session fixation, missing CSRF protection

Error_Strategy: Send error frames to client; close connection with appropriate close codes; log server-side errors
Concurrency_Strategy: Hub pattern: one goroutine per connection for read/write; broadcast via hub goroutine
Testing_Strategy: Test with websocket test client; load test concurrent connections; test reconnection logic
Anti_Patterns: Blocking the write goroutine, no ping/pong heartbeat, unbounded send buffers, not handling connection drops

Package_Priority: io, os, filepath, errgroup, minio-go or s3, log/slog
Error_Strategy: Per-file error tracking; continue batch on individual failures; report summary
Concurrency_Strategy: Bounded worker pool for parallel file processing; stream large files to avoid OOM
Testing_Strategy: Test with temp directories and fixture files; test edge cases like empty files and large files
Anti_Patterns: Reading entire large files into memory, not cleaning up temp files, ignoring file permissions, no file type validation

Package_Priority: golang-jwt/jwt/v5, bcrypt, chi, pgx, log/slog, otel
Error_Strategy: Never leak auth details in errors; generic messages to client; detailed internal logging
Concurrency_Strategy: Request-scoped; rate limit login attempts; background token cleanup goroutine
Testing_Strategy: Test token generation/validation; test password hashing; integration test full auth flow
Anti_Patterns: Storing plaintext passwords, JWT with none algorithm, no token expiration, leaking user existence in error messages

Error_Strategy: Idempotent error handling; retry transient gateway errors; alert on payment failures
Concurrency_Strategy: Sequential for payment flow; background reconciliation workers
Testing_Strategy: Mock payment gateway in tests; test webhook signature verification; test idempotency
Anti_Patterns: Storing raw card numbers, non-idempotent charges, ignoring webhook verification, missing audit trail

Error_Strategy: Retry transient failures with backoff; track delivery status; DLQ for permanent failures
Concurrency_Strategy: Worker pool per channel (email/sms/push); priority queues for urgent notifications
Testing_Strategy: Test template rendering; mock external providers; integration test delivery pipeline
Anti_Patterns: Sending notifications synchronously in request path, no rate limiting to providers, no unsubscribe mechanism

Package_Priority: olivere/elastic or typesense-go, chi, log/slog, otel
Error_Strategy: Fallback to degraded results on search engine failure; timeout queries
Concurrency_Strategy: Background indexer goroutines; bounded concurrent search queries
Testing_Strategy: Test query building; integration test with testcontainers elasticsearch; test relevance
Anti_Patterns: Re-indexing entire dataset on every change, unbounded query complexity, not paginating results, missing search analytics

Package_Priority: gorilla/websocket, chi, pgx, redis, protobuf, log/slog
Error_Strategy: Graceful degradation on storage failure; notify client of delivery status; retry sends
Concurrency_Strategy: Hub pattern for room management; goroutine per connection; Redis PubSub for multi-node
Testing_Strategy: Test message delivery; test room join/leave; load test concurrent users; test message ordering
Anti_Patterns: Storing all messages in memory, no message ordering guarantees, blocking broadcasts, no connection cleanup on disconnect

Error_Strategy: Pass through backend errors with correlation ID; circuit break on backend failures
Concurrency_Strategy: High-concurrency reverse proxy; connection pooling per backend; rate limiter goroutine
Testing_Strategy: Test routing rules; test rate limiting; test circuit breaker state transitions; load test
Anti_Patterns: Single point of failure without HA, no circuit breakers, unbounded connections to backends, not propagating tracing headers

Package_Priority: prometheus/client_golang, grafana-api, chi, html/template, embed, log/slog
Error_Strategy: Degrade gracefully when metrics source unavailable; cache last known good state
Concurrency_Strategy: Background collectors on intervals; fan-in metrics from multiple sources
Testing_Strategy: Test metric collection; test alert rule evaluation; test dashboard rendering
Anti_Patterns: Collecting metrics too frequently, unbounded metric cardinality, alerting without deduplication, no dashboard caching

Package_Priority: database/sql, pgx, encoding/csv, encoding/json, errgroup, log/slog
Error_Strategy: Per-record error tracking; checkpoint on batch boundaries; resumable on failure
Concurrency_Strategy: Fan-out extract to bounded transform workers; sequential load with batch inserts
Testing_Strategy: Test transforms as pure functions; integration test full pipeline with fixture data; test resume from checkpoint
Anti_Patterns: Loading entire dataset into memory, no checkpointing, silent data corruption, ignoring schema changes between runs
